exercises.code:
# k2 explorer · S29-T1-A-E2
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('create table p(score real check(score between 0 and 1))')
try:
    c.execute('insert into p values (1.5)')
except sqlite3.IntegrityError:
    k2_out = 'bad_score'
    print(k2_out)

exercises.code:
# k2 explorer · S29-T2-A-E1
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.executescript('create table pairs(id text); create table dec(pair_id text); insert into pairs values ("p1"),("p2"); insert into dec values ("p1");')
print([r[0] for r in c.execute('select id from pairs where id not in (select pair_id from dec)')])

# k2 explorer · S29-T3-A-E1
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')

# k2 explorer · S29-T3-B-E1
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('create table e(id text primary key, name text)')
c.execute("insert into e values ('1','A')")

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Self-check explorer S29 Q1: «Append-only en decisions significa:». Elijo índice 2 («Nueva fila por cambio de decisión») porque la teoría del slim/quiz_card («claves, constraints y normalización» **PK/FK** anclan integridad: un par referencia dos entidades. **Constraints** CHECK (score 0..1), UNIQ) alinea ese concepto; descarto p.ej. ['UPDATE del label in place sin rastro', 'Drop de tabla cada noche']. Solo material del paquete activo agentic_K2, sin claves de intentos previos.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Ser atómicas en la misma transacción lógica
